description: Hardcoded session path: PAPER = '/sessions/friendly-keen-curie/mnt/PAPER'
why_it_matters: Figure-generation script fails in any new session or on the user's machine; Figures 2-5 and Appendix F.1/F.2 cannot be regenerated.
recommended_fix: Replace with PAPER = str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2])

description: Hardcoded session path: FIG_DIR = '/sessions/friendly-keen-curie/mnt/PAPER/paper/figures/'
why_it_matters: Paper v8 docx cannot be rebuilt outside the original session.
recommended_fix: Replace with const FIG_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, 'figures') + '/'

description: Figure 1 hard-codes REGIME_LABELS as {0: 'High-Stress',...,3: 'Bull/Low-Vol'} which matches regime_labels_full.parquet (non-canonical) but is opposite of every other paper element (Tables IV-VIII, METHODOLOGY.md, model_backtest_summary.md which use 0=Low-Vol, 3=Stress).
why_it_matters: Reader confusion; future regeneration of regime_labels_full.parquet would silently invert the figure.
recommended_fix: Regenerate regime_labels_full.parquet with canonical permutation (state 0 = lowest VIX) AND update REGIME_LABELS dict accordingly. OR document the inversion prominently.

description: Hardcoded summary narrative: '(~100-160% annually) vs static CVaR (~10%)' — actual values are 225.75% / 21.40%
why_it_matters: Every rerun of script 07 overwrites panel_b_regime_oos_summary.md with wrong numbers; readers of the auto-generated MD see wrong text.
recommended_fix: Parameterise narrative on g0.loc[...,'ann_to_pct'] values pulled from the CSV.

description: CVaRConfig.max_weight default = 0.35; methodology and every active script use 0.25
why_it_matters: Future callers of solve_cvar without explicit config would silently relax the cap to 35%.
recommended_fix: Change default to 0.25 OR add a runtime assert.

description: Auto-generated conclusion lands on 'unstable' branch ('promote lag6 variant') because conclusion_stable = max_regime_diff < 0.02 but actual max_regime_diff = +0.068. This contradicts the paper's Section VII.A stance.
why_it_matters: Internal narrative inconsistency. The paper says 'main conclusion unchanged'; the auto-generated MD says 'promote'.
recommended_fix: Edit scripts/10_hicp_lag6_robustness.py:618 threshold to a more appropriate value (e.g. 0.10 or a bootstrap-CI-based threshold).

description: Named PDF is BYTE-IDENTICAL to paper_draft_JF_style_v7_CLEAN.pdf (MD5 bed0e01899a31724). It is the v7 build under a friendly name, NOT a v8 build.
why_it_matters: A reviewer who downloads the named PDF gets the v7 draft instead of v8.
recommended_fix: Delete OR regenerate as a v8 build with the friendly name.

description: Figure 3 datapoints are HARD-CODED in the script (11 (TO%, Sharpe) tuples) rather than read from CSV
why_it_matters: All 11 currently match source CSVs but any future rerun of underlying experiments would silently desync Figure 3.
recommended_fix: Refactor fig3() to load values from panel_b_regime_oos_tc_sensitivity.csv, tc_aware_cvar/tc_sensitivity.csv, regime_constraints/tc_sensitivity.csv.

description: Figure 4 bar heights are HARD-CODED in the script
why_it_matters: Future regeneration risk; same as P1-08.
recommended_fix: Refactor fig4() to compute average weights from weights_static_cvar_baseline.parquet (baseline) and panel-B weights from the FI-expanded universe.

description: Dead code: run_cvar_backtest() still encodes the OLD pipeline (full-history equal-weight fallback, embedded TC logic). Not called by any active script.
why_it_matters: Future caller could re-introduce stale methodology silently.
recommended_fix: Delete the function OR add a deprecation warning at module import.

description: State VIX z-score table claims State 0=-0.96, ..., State 3=+1.85 but actual WF-canonical state means are State 0=-0.71, ..., State 3=+1.02
why_it_matters: Descriptive table presents wrong numbers attributed to the WF labels.
recommended_fix: Update with WF-canonical means: 0=-0.71, 1=-0.40, 2=+0.17, 3=+1.02.

description: Three stale paths: reports/statistical_tests.md, reports/macro_release_date_risk_audit.md, data/processed/canonical_backtest_returns.parquet
why_it_matters: Broken links to non-existent or archived files.
recommended_fix: Update to reports/panels/panel_{a,b}_statistical_tests.md, reports/regimes/macro_release_date_risk_audit.md; remove canonical_backtest reference.

description: Does not flag regime_labels_full.parquet as orphan (no active producer). It is used by Figure 1.
why_it_matters: User running RUN_ORDER from a clean state cannot regenerate Figure 1.
recommended_fix: Add note in 'Archived (do not cite or use)' section or move regime_labels_full to data/static/ with documentation.

description: No package.json, no pinned docx version, no node_modules in repo
why_it_matters: External rebuild requires guessing 'npm install docx' version.
recommended_fix: Add paper/package.json with pinned docx@^9 (or whatever version was used).

description: Module docstring says 'Usage: python scripts/02_fit_hmm.py' — this is the ARCHIVED script name
why_it_matters: Misleading docstring; the active script is 02_hmm_walkforward_156.py.
recommended_fix: Update docstring.

description: write_validation_reports writes to config.reports_dir (= reports/) — these auto-generated CSVs/MDs would land at top-level reports/ when script 01 reruns; their current home is reports/archive/ and reports/data/
why_it_matters: Pollutes the active reports directory on next data pipeline rerun.
recommended_fix: Either point writes to reports/archive/quality/ or to reports/data/. Add explicit path constants.

description: Contains Spanish-language author preference / biographical info
why_it_matters: If repo goes public, this archive doc leaks author preferences.
recommended_fix: Delete the file or anonymise before public release.

description: 3 archived scripts contain ROOT = Path(r'c:/Users/Jorge/OneDrive - UFV/BAINF/PAPER')
why_it_matters: Personal Windows path in archive scripts.
recommended_fix: Delete archived inventory generators or replace with relative path.

description: .gitignore does NOT exclude data/raw/, data/investable_assets/, data/regime_variables/
why_it_matters: For a public release, LSEG-licensed raw xlsx would be uploaded to GitHub.
recommended_fix: Add the three directories to .gitignore (only for public-release config).

description: Two generations of weight parquets coexist (older pre-correction Apr-10, newer Apr-12). Older 5 files are no longer regenerated.
why_it_matters: Confusing co-existence of pre- and post-correction artefacts; risk of someone loading the wrong file.
recommended_fix: Move pre-correction files to data/processed/model_improvement/regime_constraints/_archive/.

description: tc_aware_cvar/performance.csv mixes 'full-history grid' (n=807) and panel-B grid (n=808) Static CVaR without a column scope flag
why_it_matters: Reader risk of confusing the 0.553/0.530 scopes.
recommended_fix: Add a 'scope' column to performance.csv ('full_history' / 'label_intersected').

description: EVAL_START = pd.Timestamp('2010-10-15') is hard-coded
why_it_matters: Mismatches if HMM warm-up window changes.
recommended_fix: Anchor to first valid label date dynamically (lab.dropna().index.min() + MIN_HISTORY).

description: Inline weighted-CVaR LP duplicates src/optimization/cvar.weighted_cvar_weights; the inline version is canonical, library is dead
why_it_matters: Risk of library version being called and producing different results.
recommended_fix: Remove the unused library version OR refactor scripts to use the library version.

description: Asset universe is hard-coded by string equality. If returns parquet renames a column the script silently treats that asset as 'neither equity nor defensive'.
why_it_matters: Future regression risk.
recommended_fix: Add an assert that EQUITY_ASSETS ∪ REAL_ESTATE ∪ DEFENSIVE_ASSETS equals the risky-asset column set.

description: Only 4 strategies (rc_baseline, rc_zew, static_cvar). Naive regime_cvar_A and weighted_cvar average weights are not exported.
why_it_matters: Cannot show per-regime weights for naive regime variants from this CSV.
recommended_fix: Extend script 15 to also export naive variants.

description: All Table values are hard-coded JS literals; no CSV ingestion
why_it_matters: Every rerun of any backtest requires hand-editing the JS source.
recommended_fix: Refactor to load CSV via fs.readFileSync + papaparse OR build via a Python wrapper that emits a values.json read by build_paper_v8.js.

description: Stale comment '(Diebold-Mariano, etc.)' on statsmodels line; methodology no longer uses DM
why_it_matters: Misleading comment.
recommended_fix: Update to 'HAC/Newey-West regressions, etc.' OR remove statsmodels entirely (it is not imported by any active script).

description: Reference MIN_TRAIN_OBS=260 as active; this is historical now (active is 156)
why_it_matters: Reader could mistake these for current methodology.
recommended_fix: Add a header note: 'Historical audit; canonical MIN_TRAIN is now 156 (see docs/METHODOLOGY.md).'

description: hmmlearn version range >=0.3,<1 — RNG behaviour could change across minor versions
why_it_matters: Bit-exact reproducibility risk.
recommended_fix: Pin exact hmmlearn version (e.g. ==0.3.x).

line_or_location: No Dockerfile, no environment.yml, no Makefile
description: Setup is documented narratively only.
why_it_matters: Add a minimal Dockerfile or conda environment.yml for one-command setup.

line_or_location: Magic constants (MIN_TRAIN_OBS, N_STATES, VIX_IDX, WALK_FORWARD_STEP) are duplicated
description: Drift risk if any of these changes.
why_it_matters: Create src/models/hmm_config.py with shared constants.

description: Drift risk; code duplication.
why_it_matters: Create src/evaluation/portfolio.py with shared helpers.

description: freq=1 excluded due to 45s sandbox timeout
why_it_matters: Mild reproducibility gap.
recommended_fix: Document that freq=1 requires a non-sandbox runtime.

description: Orphan parquet not produced by any active script; not used by any active script
why_it_matters: Confusing presence in active dir.
recommended_fix: Move to data/processed/archive/ OR add to ACTIVE_OUTPUTS.md as a static input.

description: Reads raw fixed_income xlsx files (including rejected BBG/ECB) and writes their metadata to metadata.yaml
why_it_matters: Metadata yaml contains rejected files.
recommended_fix: Filter discover_data_files to exclude data/raw/fixed_income/ from regime/investable validation.

description: Stage 7 'can be run in any order' is not literally true — scripts 12, 13, 14, 15 depend on script 11 (ZEW-swap labels)
why_it_matters: Misleading for reproducer.
recommended_fix: Add dependency note: 'scripts 12-15 read both baseline labels (from script 02) and ZEW-swap labels (from script 11)'.

description: Missing_pct column unit is fractional (0.073 for 0.073%) but other panel CSVs use different units
why_it_matters: Cosmetic inconsistency.
recommended_fix: Standardise to either fractional or percentage with explicit unit column.

description: All cells stored as string-formatted percentages ('6.42'), not numeric
why_it_matters: Awkward for downstream processing.
recommended_fix: Save numeric, format only at display time.

description: FI_FILES and FI_RENAMES dicts are duplicative (every key maps to itself)
why_it_matters: Code duplication.
recommended_fix: Collapse to a single dict.

description: Only gamma=0.5 tested
why_it_matters: Sparse grid for sensitivity check.
recommended_fix: Add gamma=0.7 or 0.3 to confirm robustness.

description: Uses Student-t df=n-1 for HAC tests; asymptotic normal would be cleaner for n>>30
why_it_matters: Conservative; negligible numerical difference.
recommended_fix: Switch to scipy.stats.norm for HAC p-values.

description: Statistical-test routines (Newey-West, block bootstrap) duplicated inline
why_it_matters: Code duplication.
recommended_fix: Refactor into src/evaluation/.

description: P2.8 (HAC arithmetic/geometric explanation) and P2.11 (Fig 3 reference line) are 'Known Remaining Issues' deferred for journal submission
why_it_matters: Pre-existing documentation; not a new finding.
recommended_fix: Address before journal submission, not before professor feedback.

description: Brief regime characterisation file; not audited deeply here
why_it_matters: Low risk.
recommended_fix: Spot-check for staleness.

description: data_validation_summary.md is written to reports/data/ via the data pipeline but is also referenced from reports/archive/. Two homes for the same file name.
why_it_matters: Filename ambiguity if reports/data_validation_summary.md is later regenerated.
recommended_fix: Standardise to one location.

description: Byte-identical copy of reports/figures/full_sample_regime_timeline.png; copy is manual
why_it_matters: Two paths for the same artefact.
recommended_fix: Symlink or document the copy step in RUN_ORDER.

description: No explicit note about scripts/07_figures/generate_paper_figures.py hardcoded path
why_it_matters: User runs it and gets confused.
recommended_fix: Add a warning under Stage 6 about the path edit needed.

line_or_location: Project uses simple half-abs-sum turnover; some papers use full turnover. Conventions match throughout but a brief footnote could clarify.
why_it_matters: Add footnote in METHODOLOGY.md

line_or_location: Project uses Newey-West with Bartlett kernel; some referees prefer the Quadratic Spectral kernel for HAC
why_it_matters: Optional robustness check

description: Resume validation guard 'label_switched is True' is unconditional; never invalidates
recommended_fix: Cosmetic; not a real risk

description: characterise_regimes heuristic for ACUTE_STRESS uses delta_US_10Y_Yield_1w which is NOT in the active 8-feature set
recommended_fix: Unreachable branch in heuristic; document or remove

why_it_matters: Add LICENSE before public release
